project.name: sdd-repo
project.description: SDD (Spec-Driven Development) Plugin for Claude Code. A monorepo implementing spec-driven development: users declare specs, agents find gaps and close them through a structured pipeline.

nodes.name: AgentChip.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the AgentChip component, covering rendering of agent avatars, name display, and size variants.

nodes.name: AgentChip.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders a small agent avatar chip displaying the agent's icon or initials, used throughout the Hub UI to indicate which agent owns or is working on an item.

nodes.name: AgentChip
nodes.summary: Exported React component rendering a visual chip for an agent with configurable size.

nodes.name: ArchiveFooter.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Comprehensive test suite for the ArchiveFooter component, covering filtering by status and domain, search, grouping by day, and item selection interactions.

nodes.name: ArchiveFooter.tsx
nodes.summary: Collapsible archive panel listing closed work items grouped by day, with search, status filter, domain filter, and click-to-open callbacks.

nodes.name: ArchiveFooter
nodes.summary: Exported React component for the expandable archive footer; manages state for expand/collapse, search query, status filter, and domain filter, and renders grouped closed work items.

nodes.name: fmtLastClosed
nodes.summary: Formats the time since the most recently closed work item as a human-readable relative string (e.g., "2h ago").

nodes.name: groupByDay
nodes.summary: Groups and sorts closed work items into day-labelled buckets for chronological display in the archive.

nodes.name: Activity.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the Activity screen, verifying rendering of activity log lines and agent filter controls.

nodes.name: Activity.tsx
nodes.summary: Activity log screen that renders a live stream of agent output lines with per-agent filtering and a pause/resume control.

nodes.name: Activity
nodes.summary: Exported React screen component rendering a filtered, pauseable activity log of agent output lines.

nodes.name: Dashboard.tsx
nodes.summary: Top-level Dashboard screen summarising all workspaces with aggregate stats, per-workspace tiles showing agent activity, and a recent activity feed.

nodes.name: Dashboard
nodes.summary: Exported React screen rendering the full dashboard with summary stats, workspace tiles, and recent activity.

nodes.name: WorkspaceTile
nodes.summary: Renders a clickable tile for a single workspace showing its agents, last-activity timestamp, and open work counts.

nodes.name: computeTotals
nodes.summary: Aggregates counts of targets, gaps, work items, and specs across all workspaces for the summary stats row.

nodes.name: Session.tsx
nodes.summary: Session overview screen providing a full view of the active SDD pipeline state: targets by status, open gaps, active work items, and spec coverage, with navigation callbacks.

nodes.name: Session
nodes.summary: Exported React screen component that renders the full SDD session state including the pipeline, target lists, spec coverage, gaps, and work items.

nodes.name: computeNextAction
nodes.summary: Derives the recommended next SDD pipeline action from current target, gap, and work-item counts, returning a label and description.

nodes.name: Pipeline
nodes.summary: Renders the horizontal SDD pipeline stage indicator with active-stage highlighting and empty-stage styling.

nodes.name: WorkItems.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the WorkItems kanban screen, covering column rendering, card display, drawer opening, and status-based grouping.

nodes.name: WorkItems.tsx
nodes.summary: Kanban-style work items screen grouping work items into status columns (backlog, in-progress, done, abandoned) with a slide-in drawer showing full work-item detail, gap linkage, and spec context.

nodes.name: WorkItems
nodes.summary: Exported React screen rendering the kanban board; manages selected drawer state and organises work items into status-based columns.

nodes.name: WorkItemDrawer
nodes.summary: Detail drawer for an individual work item showing its description, acceptance criteria, linked gap, and spec item, with navigation links.

nodes.name: KanbanCard
nodes.summary: Renders a single kanban card for a work item with agent chip, status badge, title, and relative close time.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Central TypeScript type definitions for the Hub client, declaring interfaces for Agent, Workspace, Target, Spec, Gap, WorkItem, and related domain models consumed across all screens and components.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure type-declaration file with no runtime exports; all consumers import types with `import type` or bare named imports.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Root React component and client-side router for the SDD Hub UI. Manages WebSocket connection with exponential-backoff reconnection, fetches and distributes live SDD artifacts (targets, gaps, specs, work items) across all screens, and handles workspace selection, URL state synchronization, and command palette toggling.

nodes.name: pushUrlState
nodes.summary: Serializes current workspace ID, active tab, and selected item ID into the browser URL as query parameters using history.replaceState.

nodes.name: mapApiGap
nodes.summary: Transforms a raw API response object into a typed Gap domain model, extracting the domain abbreviation from the gap ID with a regex.

nodes.name: mapApiWorkItem
nodes.summary: Transforms a raw API response object into a typed WorkItem domain model, normalizing array fields for acceptance criteria and implementation notes.

nodes.name: mapApiTarget
nodes.summary: Transforms a raw API response object into a typed Target domain model, validating status values and computing a domain abbreviation from hyphen-separated domain names.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Main application component that owns all global state (workspace, active tab, live artifacts), establishes and maintains the WebSocket connection to the hub server, and renders the layout with Header, Sidenav, CommandPalette, and the active screen.

nodes.name: Header.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the Header component, verifying rendering of breadcrumb items, agent count display, and hub address output.

nodes.name: Header.tsx
nodes.summary: React component that renders the application top bar with a live clock (updated every second via setInterval), breadcrumb navigation, agent count badge, and hub address.

nodes.name: Header
nodes.summary: Renders the hub top bar with a real-time clock, breadcrumb trail, live agent count, and hub address; uses a setInterval effect to tick the clock every second.

nodes.name: main.tsx
nodes.summary: Vite/React application entry point that mounts the App component into the DOM root and imports global CSS tokens.

nodes.name: PluginReference.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the PluginReference screen, verifying rendering of section headings, artifact table rows, and skill list entries.

nodes.name: PluginReference.tsx
nodes.summary: Screen component that renders a scrollable SDD plugin reference guide with a sticky sidebar navigation; tracks the active section via scroll events and supports smooth anchor scrolling to sections covering artifacts, lifecycle, skills, and design decisions.

nodes.name: PluginReference
nodes.summary: Renders the full SDD plugin reference page with a two-column layout: a sticky section nav on the left that highlights the active section based on scroll position, and a content area on the right with static reference data for artifacts, lifecycle, skills, and design decisions.

nodes.name: Settings.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the Settings screen that mock the fetch API to test workspace field loading and PATCH-on-blur behavior for name, path, and description fields.

nodes.name: Settings.tsx
nodes.summary: Settings screen component that loads workspace fields on mount and persists individual field changes (name, path, description) to the server via a PATCH request on blur.

nodes.name: Settings
nodes.summary: React component that fetches and displays editable workspace settings fields; patches each field individually to the server API when the user blurs an input.

nodes.name: App.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Comprehensive integration test suite for the App component covering routing, WebSocket connectivity, workspace selection, SDD artifact display (targets, gaps, work items), and live refresh via WebSocket messages.

nodes.name: ArtifactList.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the ArtifactList component, verifying filtering, archiving, and row rendering behavior with various artifact configurations.

nodes.name: ArtifactList.tsx
nodes.summary: Generic filterable and collapsible artifact list component that renders items and archived items with tab-based filtering, used across Gaps, Targets, and similar screens.

nodes.name: ArtifactList
nodes.summary: React component that accepts generic items and archived items, renders filter tabs derived from item properties, and toggles a collapsible archived section.

nodes.name: Gaps.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the Gaps screen verifying gap list rendering, detail panel display, spec linkage, work-item association, and navigation interactions.

nodes.name: Gaps.tsx
nodes.summary: Gaps screen that displays a filterable list of SDD gap artifacts alongside a detail panel with spec item linkage, work item status, and a Python code context viewer.

nodes.name: Gaps
nodes.summary: Root Gaps screen component managing active gap selection state and delegating rendering to GapDetail and ArtifactList.

nodes.name: GapDetail
nodes.summary: Detail panel component for a single gap item showing spec item linkage, reasoning, code context, work item association, and related navigation links.

nodes.name: CodeView
nodes.summary: Renders a highlighted code context block from a gap's code location with line-number annotations.

nodes.name: highlightPython
nodes.summary: Applies rudimentary HTML-based syntax highlighting for Python source code using regex replacements for keywords, strings, and comments.

nodes.name: Targets.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the Targets screen verifying target list rendering, detail panel display, dialog turn display, and navigation interactions.

nodes.name: Targets.tsx
nodes.summary: Targets screen displaying SDD target artifacts in a master-detail layout with a list of targets and an expandable detail panel showing dialog turns, statement editing, and metadata.

nodes.name: Targets
nodes.summary: Root Targets screen component managing active target selection state and rendering the target list with ArtifactList alongside the TargetDetail panel.

nodes.name: TargetDetail
nodes.summary: Detail panel for a single target showing metadata, statement editing, dialog turns with markdown-like formatting, and a composer for new turns.

nodes.name: TargetListRow
nodes.summary: Renders a single row in the targets list showing the target title, relative timestamp, and active selection state.

nodes.name: TurnBody
nodes.summary: Renders a dialog turn body with inline markdown-like formatting (bold, code, blockquotes) applied via regex-based HTML transformations.

nodes.name: CommandPalette.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Vitest test suite for the CommandPalette component, covering search filtering, keyboard navigation, result grouping, and result ordering across targets, gaps, work items, and specs.

nodes.name: renderPalette
nodes.summary: Test helper that renders CommandPalette with merged props, providing a reusable render setup for assertions.

nodes.name: CommandPalette.tsx
nodes.summary: Full-featured command palette component with fuzzy scoring, HTML-escaped highlight markup, result grouping by kind, and keyboard navigation for searching targets, gaps, work items, and specs.

nodes.name: highlight
nodes.summary: Wraps matching substring of text in a <mark> tag using HTML-escaped query for safe inline highlighting.

nodes.name: escapeHtml
nodes.summary: Escapes HTML special characters (&, <, >, ") in a string to prevent XSS in dangerouslySetInnerHTML contexts.

nodes.name: scoreItem
nodes.summary: Scores a search result item by matching query against id, title, and body fields with weighted numeric values.

nodes.name: buildResults
nodes.summary: Builds scored search results from targets, gaps, work items, and specs — showing recent items when query is empty and scored matches otherwise, sorted by descending score.

nodes.name: groupResults
nodes.summary: Groups flat search results by their kind field and orders groups by a predefined KIND_ORDER constant.

nodes.name: CommandPalette
nodes.summary: Exported React component rendering a modal search palette with a text input, grouped results list, keyboard arrow/enter/escape handling, and highlighted match rendering.

nodes.name: StatusPill.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for the StatusPill component, verifying correct CSS class application and label text rendering for various artifact status values.

nodes.name: StatusPill.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable status badge component that maps an ArtifactStatus string to a CSS modifier class and display label, rendering a colored LED indicator alongside the status text.

nodes.name: StatusPill
nodes.summary: Exported React component that renders a styled status pill with a colored LED indicator, deriving its CSS modifier and label from a static STATUS_MAP lookup.

nodes.name: Sidenav.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Vitest test suite for Sidenav verifying DOM structure (plugin-ref pinning outside scroll area), tab count badges, connection-state dots, alert count badges, and CSS layout constraints.

nodes.name: Specs.tsx
nodes.summary: Specs screen component that displays a sidebar of spec domains and a detail panel showing spec items with associated gaps and work item reference pills, supporting in-screen navigation.

nodes.name: Specs
nodes.summary: Exported React screen component rendering a two-panel layout of spec domains and their items, with gap/work-item cross-links and navigation callbacks.

nodes.name: AttachWorkspaceDialog.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Vitest test suite for AttachWorkspaceDialog covering recent-folders fetch behavior, empty/failed responses, and POST body validation with and without a description field.

nodes.name: AttachWorkspaceDialog.tsx
nodes.summary: Modal dialog component for attaching a workspace to Hub, with path entry, native folder browser integration, SDD detection preview, and POST submission to create the workspace.

nodes.name: AttachWorkspaceDialog
nodes.summary: React component rendering the attach-workspace dialog with multi-step state (path, name, description, browsing, submitting) and fetch calls for recent-workspaces and folder browsing.

nodes.name: Sidenav.tsx
nodes.summary: Sidebar navigation component for Hub that renders workspace selection dropdown, tab navigation with count badges, connection status indicator, and a pinned plugin-reference section.

nodes.name: Sidenav
nodes.summary: React component implementing the full sidebar: workspace switcher dropdown with alert badges, scrollable nav tabs with counts, hub-overview status row, and inline AttachWorkspaceDialog integration.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-001.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report documenting that agent status never transitions to 'busy' in ws-agent.ts, violating SPEC-arch-006; closed by WI-arch-019.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-002.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report documenting that the per-spec .tests.json mapping file is not parsed by sdd-parser.ts, violating SPEC-arch-019; closed by WI-arch-020.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-003.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report documenting the absence of a Vitest JSON report parser in the server, violating SPEC-arch-020; closed by WI-arch-020.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-004.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report documenting the absence of a Maven Surefire XML report parser in the server, violating SPEC-arch-021; closed by WI-arch-020.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-005.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report documenting the missing four-state test status computation (passing/failing/missing/not-run) in parseSpecs, violating SPEC-arch-022; closed by WI-arch-020.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-006.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report documenting that the file watcher does not watch test report files sourced from .tests.json mappings, violating SPEC-arch-023; closed by WI-arch-021.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-007.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report documenting that the GET /workspaces/:id/specs response lacks the testStatus field on SpecItems, violating SPEC-arch-024; closed by WI-arch-022.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-008.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report documenting that the server port was not hardcoded to 22351 as required by SPEC-arch-008; closed by WI-arch-007.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-009.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report documenting the absence of single-instance enforcement (EADDRINUSE detection) in the server startup, violating SPEC-arch-009; closed by WI-arch-007.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-010.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report documenting the missing GET /workspaces REST endpoint in the HTTP server, violating SPEC-arch-010; closed by WI-arch-008.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-011.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report documenting the missing PATCH /workspaces/:id endpoint for partial workspace updates, violating SPEC-arch-011; closed by WI-arch-008.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-012.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report documenting the missing GET /browse-folder endpoint that left the workspace dialog browse button non-functional, violating SPEC-arch-012; closed by WI-arch-009.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-013.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report documenting that the client rendered hardcoded mock workspace data instead of fetching from GET /workspaces, violating SPEC-arch-010; closed by WI-arch-010.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-014.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report documenting the missing GET /workspaces/:id/specs endpoint and client-side reliance on mock spec data, violating SPEC-arch-013; closed by WI-arch-011.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-015.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report documenting that the SQLite driver used was node:sqlite (Node 22 built-in) instead of the spec-mandated better-sqlite3, violating SPEC-arch-003; closed by WI-arch-012.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-016.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report documenting the missing GET /workspaces/:id/targets endpoint and absence of a target parser, violating SPEC-arch-014; closed by WI-arch-013.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-017.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report with detailed evidence showing that parseTargets() omits the targets/archive/ subdirectory, so archived targets are never returned by GET /workspaces/:id/targets; violates SPEC-arch-014.
nodes.languageNotes: More detailed than other gap reports — includes Spec item quote, current/expected behaviour sections, and an inline code evidence block.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-018.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report documenting the missing GET /workspaces/:id/gaps endpoint and absence of a parseGaps function, violating SPEC-arch-015; closed by WI-arch-015.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-019.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report documenting the missing GET /workspaces/:id/work-items endpoint and absence of a parseWorkItems function, violating SPEC-arch-016; closed by WI-arch-016.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-020.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report documenting that the client WebSocket handler lacked an onmessage listener, so sdd-changed events were silently discarded and no artifact re-fetch occurred, violating SPEC-arch-018; closed by WI-arch-018.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-021.md
nodes.summary: Archived architecture gap (closed) reporting that the activity message broadcast in ws-agent.ts uses an event/detail shape instead of the kind/msg/t shape required by SPEC-arch-025. Closed by WI-arch-021.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-022.md
nodes.summary: Archived architecture gap (closed) reporting that the client WebSocket onmessage handler in App.tsx only processes sdd-changed events and ignores snapshot, update, agent-registered, and activity message types as required by SPEC-arch-026. Closed by WI-arch-022.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-023.md
nodes.summary: Archived architecture gap (closed) reporting that the WebSocket client in App.tsx has no reconnect logic — on close it only sets the disconnected state without exponential backoff as required by SPEC-arch-027. Closed by WI-arch-022.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-024.md
nodes.summary: Archived architecture gap (closed) reporting that the agent register message schema lacks a name field and the server never stores or derives an agent name, violating SPEC-arch-028's requirement for optional name storage with server-side initials derivation. Closed by WI-arch-023.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-025.md
nodes.summary: Archived architecture gap (closed) reporting that the agents database table has no name column and getAllAgents does not return name or computed initials, violating SPEC-arch-029. Closed by WI-arch-023.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-026.md
nodes.summary: Archived architecture gap (closed) reporting that GET /workspaces returns bare DbWorkspace rows without the enriched counts, agents, and lastActivity fields required by SPEC-arch-030. Closed by WI-arch-024.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-027.md
nodes.summary: Archived architecture gap (closed) reporting that the MOCK_AGENTS constant is still used in App.tsx throughout multiple component passes instead of liveAgents state populated from WebSocket messages, violating SPEC-arch-031. Closed by WI-arch-025.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-028.md
nodes.summary: Archived architecture gap (closed) reporting that POST /workspaces silently swallows duplicate-path inserts via INSERT OR IGNORE instead of returning HTTP 409 as required by SPEC-arch-033. Closed by WI-arch-026.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-029.md
nodes.summary: Archived architecture gap (closed) reporting that PATCH /workspaces/:id never stops the previous filesystem watcher or starts a new one when the workspace path changes, violating SPEC-arch-034. Closed by WI-arch-027.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-030.md
nodes.summary: Archived architecture gap (closed) reporting that parseTargets reads from the archive directory but does not override target status to 'archived', leaving frontmatter status values unchanged in violation of SPEC-arch-035. Closed by WI-arch-028.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-031.md
nodes.summary: Archived architecture gap (closed) reporting that WebSocket snapshot and update broadcasts in ws-ui.ts use getAllWorkspaces instead of an enriched variant, sending bare workspace rows without counts, agents, or lastActivity as required by SPEC-arch-036. Closed by WI-arch-029.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-032.md
nodes.summary: Archived architecture gap (closed) reporting that the server-side WorkspaceCounts interface declares only 4 of the 12 fields required by SPEC-arch-037, omitting targetsAwaitingAgent, targetsReady, targetsDraft, specs, specItems, staleAuditDomains, workPending, and workDoneToday. Closed by WI-arch-029.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-033.md
nodes.summary: Archived architecture gap (closed) reporting that the UiMessage union type in ws-ui.ts is missing AgentRegisteredMessage and ActivityMessage variants, causing broadcastRaw to be used instead of typed broadcast functions, violating SPEC-arch-038. Closed by WI-arch-030.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-034.md
nodes.summary: Archived architecture gap (closed) reporting that the GET /recent-workspaces endpoint is entirely absent from the server's handleApi router, causing the client's AttachWorkspaceDialog to receive HTML instead of JSON, violating SPEC-arch-039. Closed by WI-arch-031.

nodes.name: GAP-arch-035.md
nodes.summary: Archived architecture gap (closed) reporting that the client ArtifactStatus union type in types.ts is missing the 'deferred' and 'abandoned' values required by SPEC-arch-040, making deferred/abandoned status filtering silently ineffective. Closed by WI-arch-032.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-001.md
nodes.summary: Archived screens gap (closed) reporting that the Targets live fetch in App.tsx stores raw API response directly into liveTargets state without mapping fields to the frontend Target type as required by SPEC-scr-009. Closed by WI-scr-001.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-002.md
nodes.summary: Archived screens gap (closed) reporting that the Targets archived divider in Targets.css lacks the eyebrow-divider visual treatment with centered label flanked by full-width HR lines as required by SPEC-scr-010. Closed by WI-scr-002.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-003.md
nodes.summary: Archived screens gap (closed) reporting that the Targets composer 'mark ready' button in Targets.tsx is conditionally rendered only for awaiting-user status instead of always being present in the toolbar as required by SPEC-scr-011. Closed by WI-scr-003.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-004.md
nodes.summary: Archived screens gap (closed) reporting that the Targets statement body in Targets.css uses font-size 13px with no font-family instead of the Newsreader serif at 16px with line-height 1.55 required by SPEC-scr-013. Closed by WI-scr-004.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-005.md
nodes.summary: Archived screens gap (closed) reporting that the Targets list in Targets.css uses the wrong background CSS variable and lacks the 0.55 opacity treatment for archived rows required by SPEC-scr-014. Closed by WI-scr-005.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-006.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report documenting that the Targets archived divider is not collapsible, has incorrect label letter-spacing and font-weight, and renders the archived count outside a monospace span — all closed against SPEC-scr-015.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-007.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report recording wrong padding on the Targets dialog, incorrect WHO label font-size, and incorrect bubble padding in Targets.css — closed against SPEC-scr-016.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-008.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report noting that the PluginReference screen is entirely absent from the codebase — no component file, no App.tsx import, and no sidenav registration — closed against SPEC-scr-017.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-009.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report capturing that App.tsx hardcodes tabCounts as an empty object rather than computing live counts from targets, gaps, and work items, leaving sidenav badges always empty — closed against SPEC-scr-018.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-010.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report documenting that the CommandPalette component is entirely missing — no ⌘K handler, no overlay dialog, no grouped results or keyboard navigation — closed against SPEC-scr-019.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-011.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report identifying that active target rows inherit an off-white container background from .targets-list rather than rendering with #ffffff as specified — closed against SPEC-scr-012.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-012.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report noting that App.tsx passes MOCK_TARGETS to the Targets screen instead of fetching live data from the backend — closed against SPEC-scr-009.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-013.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report capturing that the archived divider in Targets.tsx is missing its trailing dot and the flanking horizontal rule lines required by the spec — closed against SPEC-scr-010.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-014.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report documenting that active target rows in Targets.css use an off-white background variable instead of the spec-required #ffffff — closed against SPEC-scr-012.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-015.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report noting that dialog turn bubble font-size in Targets.css is 13px instead of the spec-required 14px — closed against SPEC-scr-016.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-016.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report recording that the Gaps screen renders mock data rather than fetching live data from the backend — closed against SPEC-scr-020.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-017.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report noting that the WorkItems screen renders mock data rather than fetching live data from the backend — closed against SPEC-scr-021.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-018.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report capturing that the selected workspace ID is not persisted to localStorage in App.tsx, breaking workspace selection across reloads — closed against SPEC-scr-022.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-019.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report documenting that App.tsx uses only localStorage for state persistence with no URLSearchParams read on mount or history.replaceState calls, so workspace, tab, and item selections are not URL-restorable — closed against SPEC-scr-023.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-020.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report noting that selected archived target rows in ArtifactList.css lack an opacity:1 override, leaving them visually muted when selected — closed against SPEC-scr-014.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-021.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report capturing that the sidenav specs count in App.tsx is derived from mock data rather than live spec data — closed against SPEC-scr-018.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-022.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report documenting that the Gaps screen archived section filters only on 'closed' status, omitting deferred and accepted items as required by the spec — closed against SPEC-scr-020.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-023.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report noting that Targets.tsx bypasses the shared ArtifactList component when any filter tab other than 'all' is active, re-implementing row rendering inconsistently — closed against SPEC-scr-025.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-024.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report recording that MOCK_SPECS items in App.tsx are missing the required testStatus field, causing TypeScript type errors — closed against SPEC-scr-027.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-025.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report capturing that the Dashboard receives MOCK_WORKSPACES instead of live WorkspaceData from GET /workspaces — closed against SPEC-scr-028.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-026.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report noting that the Activity screen received static MOCK_ACTIVITY data instead of accumulating real WebSocket events in a liveActivity state, violating SPEC-scr-029. Closed by WI-scr-026.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-027.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report identifying that the CommandPalette onNavigate callback discarded the selected item ID and never called setSelectedItemId, violating SPEC-scr-030's requirement to pre-select the artifact in the target tab. Closed by WI-scr-027.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-028.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report documenting that screen components (Targets, Specs, Gaps, WorkItems) were rendered without forwarding the URL-parsed selectedItemId as their initialXxxId prop, violating SPEC-scr-031. Closed by WI-scr-028.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-029.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report recording that the TargetStatus union type was missing the 'archived' value and Targets.tsx incorrectly used 'accepted' as the archive filter, violating SPEC-scr-032. Closed by WI-scr-029.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-030.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report identifying leftover MOCK_SPECS, MOCK_GAPS, and MOCK_WORK_ITEMS constants in App.tsx that were still wired into four render sites instead of live server-derived state, violating SPEC-scr-033. Closed by WI-scr-030.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-031.md
nodes.summary: Archived gap report noting that Activity.tsx contained LIVE_EXTRAS fake data, a tickCount state, and a setInterval generating synthetic activity lines that had to be deleted per SPEC-scr-034. Closed by WI-scr-031.

nodes.name: GAP-scr-032.md

nodes.name: SPEC-architecture.md
nodes.summary: Canonical architecture spec defining 41 spec items (SPEC-arch-001 through SPEC-arch-041) that govern the Hub's server runtime, persistence layer, WebSocket protocol, REST API endpoints, filesystem watcher, test report parsing, and client reconnection behavior.

nodes.name: SPEC-ui-components.md
nodes.summary: Canonical UI components spec defining 10 spec items (SPEC-uic-001 through SPEC-uic-010) that govern shared React components including ArtifactList, ArchiveFooter, TestStatusDot, AgentChip, StatusPill, and their CSS encapsulation and filtering pipeline rules.

nodes.name: SPEC-ui-layout.md
nodes.summary: Canonical UI layout spec defining 17 spec items (SPEC-ui-001 through SPEC-ui-017) that govern the application shell's three-region layout, sidenav, header, main content scrolling, workspace selector, attach dialog, and visual treatment.

nodes.name: SPEC-ui-screens.md
nodes.summary: Canonical UI screens spec defining 37 spec items (SPEC-scr-001 through SPEC-scr-037) that govern individual Hub screens — Dashboard, Session, Targets, Specs, Gaps, WorkItems, Settings, Activity, PluginReference — including live data fetching, URL state, and command palette behavior.

nodes.name: SPEC-workflow.md
nodes.summary: Canonical workflow spec defining 12 spec items (SPEC-wf-001 through SPEC-wf-012) that govern the SDD two-phase workflow, sdd-worker spawning via TeamCreate, skill pipeline footers, and statusline configuration for tracking pipeline state.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive design handoff document for SDD Hub covering visual language, typography, color tokens, spacing, component guidelines, screen-by-screen layout specs, interaction patterns, state management, and implementation suggestions across 28 sections.

nodes.name: SDD Hub - Terminal.html
nodes.summary: High-fidelity HTML prototype of the SDD Hub UI in a phosphor-on-graphite terminal aesthetic, loading React and screen components via Babel in-browser with mocked data; provided as a reference exploration rather than the approved build direction.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Babel standalone and UMD React loaded from CDN, enabling JSX in-browser without a build step — a purely static design-review artifact.

nodes.name: SDD Hub.html
nodes.summary: High-fidelity HTML prototype of the approved SDD Hub UI in the editorial-minimalist direction (pure white, neutral charcoal, oxblood accent), loading React screen components via Babel in-browser with mocked SDD data.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Newsreader, Geist, and JetBrains Mono from Google Fonts — the three-font typographic system defined in the design spec.

nodes.name: styles-terminal.css
nodes.summary: Design system stylesheet for the terminal-variant SDD Hub UI, defining CSS custom properties for a phosphor-green-on-graphite color palette, monospace typography tokens, spacing scale, and component styles for the full screen set.
nodes.languageNotes: At 999 lines this is a complete standalone design system in CSS custom properties — no preprocessor, all tokens defined at :root for the terminal phosphor palette.

nodes.name: styles.css
nodes.summary: Primary design system stylesheet for the approved SDD Hub editorial UI, defining the full CSS token set for the white-background, neutral-grey, oxblood-accent visual language plus component styles for all screens including sidebar, status pills, buttons, and activity feed.
nodes.languageNotes: At 1220 lines this is the canonical design-system stylesheet; CSS custom properties at :root encode the approved color, type, and spacing tokens that the hub client should mirror in its implementation.

tour.title: The Big Idea: SDD
tour.description: Start here to understand why this project exists. The README articulates the core shift: instead of telling an agent what to do, you declare what must be true, and the agent finds where reality diverges from the declaration and closes the gaps. It maps the full six-step pipeline — write a target, negotiate it, fold it into a spec, audit the codebase, decompose gaps into work items, close them — and introduces the four artifact types that carry state through that pipeline.

tour.title: Plugin Registration
tour.description: The plugin manifest is what makes SDD skills available inside Claude Code. It declares the plugin's name, version, and author and acts as the registry entry point that the plugin loader reads to discover every skill, command, and hook the SDD system provides. Understanding this file tells you the full surface area of the plugin before diving into any individual skill.

tour.title: The SDD Workflow Skills
tour.description: These SKILL.md files define the executable workflow: session-start surfaces urgency-ordered status; target-engage negotiates and folds a target into a spec; spec-audit walks the codebase against a spec domain and produces gap files; gap-to-work-items decomposes those gap reports into scoped tasks; and work-item-close implements and verifies a single task. Together they implement every step of the pipeline described in the README, in an order that directly mirrors it.

tour.title: The Spec Files: Source of Truth
tour.description: The specs in .sdd/specs/ are what the codebase is audited against. SPEC-workflow.md defines the 12 contract items governing the SDD pipeline itself; SPEC-architecture.md defines the 41 structural invariants for the Hub server and client. These files are the authoritative declaration of what must be true — the foundation that gives spec-audit its checklist and that makes gap reports meaningful rather than arbitrary.

tour.title: Hub Server Entry Point
tour.description: The hub server is the runtime backbone of the SDD dashboard. hub/server/index.ts bootstraps an HTTP server with Express-style routing, exposing REST endpoints for workspaces and agents, and wires up both the UI WebSocket handler and the agent WebSocket handler. It also initialises the SQLite database and the filesystem watcher on startup, so this single file is the dependency graph root for the entire backend.

tour.title: The SDD Parser
tour.description: sdd-parser.ts is the most fan-out-heavy file in the server (19 outbound edges), making it the intellectual core of the backend. It reads raw markdown files from .sdd/ and parses them into strongly-typed artifact objects — targets, specs, gaps, and work items — by extracting YAML frontmatter and structured content. Every downstream subsystem that needs artifact data consumes this parser's output rather than reading files directly.

tour.title: Filesystem Watcher
tour.description: watcher.ts uses chokidar to monitor the .sdd/ directories for changes and fires debounced callbacks whenever a markdown file is created, modified, or deleted. It is the reactive heart of the server: when a work-item-close skill writes a file to disk, the watcher detects the change, re-parses the artifact, and triggers the WebSocket broadcast that updates every connected dashboard in real time without any polling.

tour.title: WebSocket Handlers
tour.description: Two parallel WebSocket handlers serve two very different audiences. ws-ui.ts serves the browser dashboard: it sends a full workspace snapshot on connect and broadcasts incremental updates whenever artifacts change. ws-agent.ts serves Claude agent sessions: it receives agent registration and heartbeat messages, tracks agent status (idle/busy), and relays activity events to any listening UI clients. This separation means the browser and agents speak different protocols over the same WebSocket upgrade endpoint.

tour.title: Database Layer
tour.description: The SQLite persistence layer (db/index.ts + db/schema.ts) stores workspace registrations and agent records using better-sqlite3. schema.ts declares the CREATE TABLE DDL strings for workspaces and agents; index.ts wraps them in typed CRUD functions. With fan-out of 14, db/index.ts is imported by almost every server module that needs to read or write persistent state, making it the server's shared data contract.

tour.title: Hub Client Entry Point
tour.description: hub/client/src/main.tsx is the Vite/React entry point: it mounts the App component into the DOM root and imports the global CSS. App.tsx (fan-out of 21, the highest in the codebase) is the client's central hub — it manages the WebSocket connection with exponential-backoff reconnection, owns the top-level application state, and renders the client-side router that maps URL paths to screen components. Every screen and navigation element the user sees flows from this file.

tour.title: Shared Type Vocabulary
tour.description: hub/client/src/types.ts has a fan-in of 27 — more than any other file in the project — meaning almost every component and screen imports from it. It declares the TypeScript interfaces for Agent, Workspace, Target, Gap, WorkItem, Spec, and ArtifactStatus that form the shared vocabulary between the WebSocket messages, the screen components, and the reusable UI components. Understanding this file is prerequisite to understanding any other client file.

tour.title: Shared UI Components
tour.description: Three reusable components form the visual vocabulary used on every screen. StatusPill (fan-in 11) maps an ArtifactStatus string to a styled badge — the universal status indicator. ArtifactList (fan-in 5) is a generic filterable and collapsible list used on the Targets, Gaps, and WorkItems screens alike. AgentChip (fan-in 8) renders an agent's avatar or initials wherever agent identity is shown. These components enforce visual consistency and eliminate duplicated rendering logic across the application.

tour.title: The Targets and Gaps Screens
tour.description: Targets.tsx and Gaps.tsx are the two most data-rich screens and the clearest examples of the full data flow. Targets.tsx renders SDD target artifacts in a master-detail layout — a list of targets on the left, a detail panel on the right — using ArtifactList as the list substrate and StatusPill for each item's status badge. Gaps.tsx follows the same pattern but adds spec cross-reference panels. Together they show how WebSocket data from App.tsx flows through types.ts interfaces into screen props and finally into reusable components.

tour.title: Design System
tour.description: The visual language of the Hub dashboard is rooted in two files. tokens.css defines CSS custom properties for the full color palette, typography scale, spacing system, and elevation — every component's visual style is expressed using these variables rather than hard-coded values. The design README in .sdd/specs/design/ is the design handoff document that describes the intent behind each token group, the naming conventions, and the rules for using dark/light mode tokens correctly.

tour.title: The SDD Artifact Archive
tour.description: The .sdd/ archive directories are the historical record of the project's spec-driven development cycle applied to itself. GAP-arch-001.md is a closed gap report documenting a real divergence that was found (agent status never transitioning to 'busy' in ws-agent.ts) and then resolved by a work-item-close cycle. Reading one archived gap alongside its originating spec item and closed work item gives you a concrete, end-to-end example of the entire SDD pipeline running on the very codebase that implements it.
